"A suit which laboring men and
Particularly members of labor unions
will watch with interest has been
commenced In the superior court.
The suit is entitled Maxwell & Hol-
lingsbury VS.. Edward Evans ct al,
and Is but another chapter in an af-
fair which led Charles Gaebler to
bring suit against the same parties a
short time ago.


Gaebler was n member of the Spo-
kane Master Plumbers association
and was in the employ of Maxwell &
Hollingsbury. For some cause Gaeb-
Ter was blacklisted by the associa-
ton and his employers were com
PEELED to discharge him. For this
blacklisting Gaebler brought suit for
damages against the members of the
association.


Later 1t was alleged that Maxwell
4 Hollingsbury gave information to
Gaebler of the doings of tho associa-
ton and they We're compelled to
withdraw from the association, their
names were placed on the blacklist
and dealers In plumbers supplies In
the city were notified not to sell them
goods.


This suit IL brought for the pur-
pose of securing an injunction re-
straining the Master Plumbers asso-
citation from keeping the name OF the
plaintiffs on the blacklist and to COM
pei II to rescind the order prohibiting
dealers in plumbers supplies selling
goods to plaintiffs. 90.
